prompt: What new AI primitives are emerging in 2026 that could become large categories: verifiable inference, agent payments (x402/stablecoin), agent identity/auth, long-term memory, code sandboxes, simulation/world models, on-device agents, AI-native databases? State of each, who is building, and how big the opening is. Cite sources.
content:
**These AI primitives are emerging as foundational building blocks for scalable, autonomous agentic systems in 2026.** They address key limitations in current AI (e.g., trust, economics, persistence, safety, and efficiency). Many are moving from research/prototypes into early production, driven by the shift from chatbots to agents that act, transact, and persist. Below is a summary based on recent developments (as of mid-2026), including state of maturity, key builders, and estimated opportunity scale.[[1]](https://www.elibrary.imf.org/view/journals/068/2026/004/article-A001-en.xml)[[2]](https://eco.com/support/en/articles/14839401-agentic-payments-how-ai-agents-pay)

### Verifiable Inference
**State**: Early production for high-stakes uses (e.g., DeFi, finance, compliance). Enables cryptographic proofs (via ZKML/zk-SNARKs) that a specific model/weights were used on given inputs without tampering or revealing weights. Not yet mainstream due to proof-generation overhead, but gaining traction for on-chain verification and agent trust.[[3]](https://equilibrium.co/writing/state-of-verifiable-inference)[[4]](https://www.agilesoftlabs.com/blog/2026/05/ai-blockchain-in-2026-5-onchain)

**Who is building**: EZKL (ONNX-to-ZK circuits, DeFi/DePIN integrations), Giza (autonomous DeFi agents), Lagrange (DeepProve library + prover network), Inference Labs (on-chain verification protocols), Ritual (infrastructure). Broader ecosystem includes NVIDIA and decentralized compute networks.[[3]](https://equilibrium.co/writing/state-of-verifiable-inference)[[5]](https://tracxn.com/d/companies/inferencelabs/__1tyE2uFLzHvAXG3MBURzwVsWzmGDt98ENJWsa6nNmqc)

**Opportunity size**: Large in regulated/financial verticals (proof value often exceeds costs); part of the broader AI inference market (projected multi-hundred-billion scale by early 2030s, with verifiable as a premium layer). Early traction in DeFi and agent security.[[6]](https://www.polarismarketresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-inference-market)

### Agent Payments (x402/Stablecoin)
**State**: Production deployments accelerating rapidly (hundreds of millions of transactions by mid-2026). x402 revives HTTP 402 for micropayments (stablecoins like USDC settled on-chain, often gasless via EIP-3009/Permit2 on Base/Solana). Complements card-based mandates (e.g., shared payment tokens). Handles agent-to-agent and agent-to-service flows at machine scale.[[7]](https://nevermined.ai/blog/stablecoin-payments-ai-agents-statistics)[[8]](https://eco.com/support/en/articles/14839404-stablecoin-payments-for-ai-agents)[[2]](https://eco.com/support/en/articles/14839401-agentic-payments-how-ai-agents-pay)

**Who is building**: Coinbase (x402 protocol, open-sourced/Linux Foundation governance), Stripe (Machine Payments Protocol/MPP on Base, Tempo integration), Google (AP2 mandates, co-designed with Mastercard/PayPal), OpenAI (Agentic Commerce Protocol/ACP + Stripe), Visa/Mastercard (Trusted Agent Protocol/Agent Pay with tokenization), Nevermined/Circle/Kite (integrations/wallets), FluxA and others.[[2]](https://eco.com/support/en/articles/14839401-agentic-payments-how-ai-agents-pay)[[9]](https://nevermined.ai/blog/stripe-vs-skyfire-vs-nevermined)[[10]](https://www.emergingfintech.co/p/the-agentic-web-inside-the-protocol)

**Opportunity size**: Massive—stablecoin volume already trillions annually; agent payments a tiny but fast-growing fraction with potential for trillions in agentic commerce/e-commerce (projections of agents handling 20%+ of tasks). New rails for micropayments and M2M economies.[[7]](https://nevermined.ai/blog/stablecoin-payments-ai-agents-statistics)[[11]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/agentic-ai-trends)

### Agent Identity/Auth
**State**: Standards and runtime enforcement emerging (post-Gartner predictions of widespread independent agents). Focus on verifiable delegation, phishing-resistant auth, ephemeral credentials, and runtime policy enforcement (beyond static IAM). NIST and FIDO initiatives active.[[12]](https://www.strata.io/blog/agentic-identity/new-identity-playbook-ai-agents-not-nhi-8b/)[[13]](https://fidoalliance.org/fido-alliance-to-develop-standards-for-trusted-ai-agent-interactions/)

**Who is building**: FIDO Alliance (Agentic Authentication Working Group), Ping Identity (Identity for AI with Agent IAM Core/Gateway), Google/Mastercard (AP2/Verifiable Intent), NIST (standards for software/AI agent identity/authorization), SPIFFE/SPIRE ecosystem, Microsoft Entra/CyberArk/Auth0 adaptations, APort and others.[[14]](https://press.pingidentity.com/2026-03-24-Ping-Identity-Defines-the-Runtime-Identity-Standard-for-Autonomous-AI)[[13]](https://fidoalliance.org/fido-alliance-to-develop-standards-for-trusted-ai-agent-interactions/)

**Opportunity size**: High—addresses “agent sprawl” and security crisis; foundational for trusted commerce/transactions. Enterprise IAM market expansion with new categories for nonhuman identities (NHIs).[[15]](https://www.iansresearch.com/resources/all-blogs/post/security-blog/2026/04/19/ai-agents-are-creating-an-identity-security-crisis-in-2026)

### Long-Term Memory
**State**: Maturing into a distinct engineering discipline with benchmarks (e.g., BEAM). Architectures separate episodic (events), semantic (facts/preferences), and procedural (workflows) memory; retrieval-augmented with consolidation/decay. Moving beyond simple vector stores to production systems.[[16]](https://mem0.ai/blog/state-of-ai-agent-memory-2026)[[17]](https://vectorize.io/articles/best-ai-agent-memory-systems)

**Who is building**: Mem0 (vector+graph, high stars/production focus), Zep, Letta, Cognee, Hindsight (top benchmarks), Cloudflare (managed Agent Memory), Redis (Agent Memory Server + MCP integration), LangMem/LangGraph integrations.[[18]](https://redis.io/blog/long-term-memory-architectures-ai-agents/)[[19]](https://blog.cloudflare.com/introducing-agent-memory/)[[17]](https://vectorize.io/articles/best-ai-agent-memory-systems)

**Opportunity size**: Significant differentiator for persistent agents; core to “agent memory” as a battleground layer in the stack. Enables personalization, long-running workflows, and institutional knowledge.[[20]](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/richmondalake_100daysofagentmemory-memoryengineering-activity-7402719428624408577-_81p)

### Code Sandboxes
**State**: Widely adopted for safe execution of agent-generated code. Ranges from lightweight isolates (microVMs) to full ephemeral containers/VMs; emphasis on isolation, cold starts, and state persistence.[[21]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/ai-agent-sandbox)[[22]](https://blaxel.ai/blog/code-execution-sandboxes-for-ai-agents)

**Who is building**: E2B (popular for code execution), Modal, Daytona, Northflank, Cloudflare (Dynamic Workers), Blaxel and others.[[21]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/ai-agent-sandbox)[[23]](https://blog.cloudflare.com/dynamic-workers/)

**Opportunity size**: Essential infrastructure for coding/reasoning agents; reduces risk in autonomous execution. Part of broader dev/agent tooling growth.[[21]](https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/ai-agent-sandbox)

### Simulation/World Models
**State**: Advancing from research to practical agent training/evaluation. World models simulate physics/environments for safe, scalable interaction (vs. real-world costs/risks). Early interactive 3D models available.[[24]](https://www.mintz.com/insights-center/events/2026/agents-world-models-why-ais-next-leap-simulation)[[25]](https://themesis.com/2026/01/07/world-models-five-competing-approaches/)

**Who is building**: Google DeepMind (Genie 3, SIMA), NVIDIA (Cosmos world foundation models), World Labs, Yann LeCun/AMI Labs efforts, academic/open research.[[26]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3Errq-0T9w0)[[25]](https://themesis.com/2026/01/07/world-models-five-competing-approaches/)

**Opportunity size**: High for robotics, AV, planning, and AGI paths—enables “experience” without real-world interaction. Bridges agents to physical/digital worlds.[[24]](https://www.mintz.com/insights-center/events/2026/agents-world-models-why-ais-next-leap-simulation)

### On-Device Agents
**State**: Expanding via optimized models on consumer hardware (phones, edge devices). Reduces latency/cloud dependence; early deployments in mobile AI and specialized devices.[[27]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y-6HBhw0L_8)

**Who is building**: Apple (Intelligence/Siri), Google (Gemini on-device), Samsung (Galaxy AI integrations), Qualcomm and chipmakers; frameworks like Droidrun for mobile automation.[[27]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y-6HBhw0L_8)[[28]](https://dev.to/priya_negi_9ffd29931ea408/best-mobile-ai-agents-in-2026-pe0)

**Opportunity size**: Broad consumer/edge market; enables privacy-focused, always-on agents. Complements cloud for hybrid architectures.[[27]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y-6HBhw0L_8)

### AI-Native Databases
**State**: Databases evolving with native AI features (vector search, in-DB ML inference, agent memory layers, automated insights). Shifting from bolt-ons to core primitives for agentic workloads.[[29]](https://reevo.ai/blog/ai-native-crm-guide)[[30]](https://stackby.com/blog/ai-database-tools/)

**Who is building**: Snowflake (Cortex), Databricks, Stackby/Baserow/Airtable (AI fields/assistants), Reevo and specialized AI CRMs/databases, vector/graph hybrids (e.g., tied to memory systems).[[29]](https://reevo.ai/blog/ai-native-crm-guide)[[30]](https://stackby.com/blog/ai-database-tools/)

**Opportunity size**: Core to data/agent stacks; supports real-time retrieval, memory, and analytics at scale. Expands traditional DB markets with AI-native demands.[[30]](https://stackby.com/blog/ai-database-tools/)

These primitives are interdependent (e.g., verifiable inference + payments + identity enable trusted agent economies; memory + sandboxes + world models support safe long-running agents). The overall opportunity is in the hundreds of billions to trillions as agentic systems scale, with standards bodies (Linux Foundation, FIDO, NIST) accelerating interoperability. Developments are fast-moving—many protocols reached production traction in 2025–early 2026.
